steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure/Function question asking what the underlined sentence does in context. The key is that it immediately follows “Day by day, Dot grew stronger” — so the underlined sentence must support or illustrate that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It offers a detail that shows how Dot is growing stronger.’: Dot bouncing for hours in her stroller to develop her leg muscles is a specific physical detail that directly illustrates the previous sentence’s claim that she is growing stronger. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘It expresses hope that Celestine will mature psychologically.’: The underlined sentence is about Dot’s physical activity, not about Celestine at all — Celestine is not mentioned in it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It explores some of the narrator's psychological characteristics.’: The underlined sentence describes Dot’s behavior in her stroller; the narrator does not appear in it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It indicates how sad Celestine is by describing a behavior she engages in.’: Dot, not Celestine, is doing the bouncing. The sentence says nothing about Celestine’s sadness. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
